title: Digital Divide Measurement And School Internet Connectivity In Low-Income Countries
summary: Build a verified workflow for measuring the school-level digital divide — internet connectivity, device access, and digital literacy — in low-income countries, linking school census data, infrastructure maps, and connectivity measurements.
why_it_matters: UNICEF and ITU estimate 1.3 billion school-age children lack home internet access and two-thirds of schools in low-income countries lack any internet connection. Digital learning cannot reach the children who need it most if schools have no connectivity. But school-level connectivity data is fragmented — some countries have detailed school census data with ICT indicators, others have nothing.

success_metrics.target: Every connectivity estimate links school-level or survey data with year and methodology.
